mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60d returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in industrial production reflects accelerating manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through short-term earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are negative in >60% of expansion periods, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates combined with declining unemployment indicate a resilient economy capable of absorbing tighter policy, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession fears subside.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20d returns in >65% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms sustained economic expansion, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are negative in >58% of periods meeting the threshold, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment gains exceeding 200,000 alongside elevated federal funds rates signal persistent inflationary pressure and higher probability of further monetary tightening, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are positive in >62% of triggered cases, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01 when CPIAUCSL is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher consumer price index levels reflect elevated inflation expectations that compress real discount rates and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are positive in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL rises, or the signal shows no statistical significance in a 10-year out-of-sample backtest.
